entities.name: ORTHODOX SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds that can be dried to low moisture contents without damage to maintain longevity

entities.name: LOW MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The state of seeds having reduced water content, specifically targeted at 5±2 per cent

entities.name: LONGEVITY
entities.description: The duration of seed viability and survival

entities.name: SEED DRYING
entities.description: The process of evaporating moisture from the seed surface into the outer atmosphere

entities.name: RELATIVE HUMIDITY
entities.description: The gradient of moisture in the air which influences the seed drying process

entities.name: SUN-DRYING
entities.description: A low-cost drying method that utilizes solar energy but can affect seed quality

entities.name: SEED QUALITY
entities.description: The physiological and genetic integrity of the seeds

entities.name: HUMID TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic regions characterized by high relative humidity where sun-drying is impractical

entities.name: COMMERCIAL SEED DRYERS
entities.description: Mechanical equipment used for the artificial drying of seeds

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL DRYING
entities.description: The process of drying seeds using controlled mechanical environments

entities.name: AIR TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal state of the air during drying which must be controlled to prevent deterioration

entities.name: SEED DETERIORATION
entities.description: The process of seed quality decline and loss of viability

entities.name: LOW TEMPERATURE-RELATIVE HUMIDITY DRYING
entities.description: A recommended drying approach using low temperature and relative humidity to achieve 5±2 per cent moisture

entities.name: GERMPLASM CONSERVATION
entities.description: The long-term preservation of genetic resources in seed form
